compressed_prompt: Write angry limericks on moms' chore kids.
response:
There once were kids given chores with a frown,
By their moms, who expected them to get down,
To work with a sigh,
And a "do I have to?" cry,
Their laziness was truly renowned.

In a house full of mess and disarray,
A mom gave her kids chores to do each day,
But they just wouldn't try,
And made a big mess to the sky,
And their mom was left to clean up the fray.

Some kids got chores, oh what a test,
Their moms thought they'd learn to be their best,
But they just played all day,
And left the work to decay,
And their moms were left to do it, and be stressed.

In a family with kids who were slow,
Their mom gave them chores, don't you know,
But they just dawdled along,
And sang a lazy song,
And their mom was left to do all the work to show.

There once were some kids with chores to abide,
Their mom thought they'd learn to be inside,
But they just rebelled with flair,
And left their work to impair,
And their mom was left to be the one to provide.
